title: UTCDate stores as transparent string and parses lazily via parse() — correct tradeoff
description: UTCDate wraps a String and exposes parse() -> Result<chrono::DateTime<Utc>, ClientError> rather than storing a parsed DateTime. A future contributor might suggest parsing eagerly during deserialization to catch malformed dates at the boundary. The lazy approach is correct for two reasons: (1) JMAP date strings round-trip exactly — the wire value is preserved verbatim for re-serialization, which eager parsing would break by reformatting; (2) most code paths that receive a UTCDate (display, comparison by string) do not need a parsed DateTime, so eager parsing would pay chrono overhead on every deserialization. The serde(transparent) attribute enforces that UTCDate serializes identically to a plain string, which is the RFC 8620 §1.4 requirement. Do not change this to eager parsing.
